title: System Monitoring & Logs
description: Learn to monitor system performance and analyze logs using journalctl, dmesg, and top.
content: Monitor RHEL system health using journalctl, dmesg, and standard Linux tools. Track CPU, memory, disk usage and identify issues from logs.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== System Uptime ==='
uptime
echo -e '\n=== Memory Usage ==='
free -h
echo -e '\n=== Disk Usage ==='
df -h
echo -e '\n=== Top 5 Processes ==='
ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -6
echo -e '\n=== Recent Errors ==='
journalctl -p err -n 10 --no-pager
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess
import psutil
from datetime import datetime

print(f'System Report - {datetime.now()}')
print(f'CPU: {psutil.cpu_percent()}%')
mem = psutil.virtual_memory()
print(f'Memory: {mem.percent}%')
disk = psutil.disk_usage('/')
print(f'Disk: {disk.percent}%')

title: Patching & Package Management
description: Master dnf, yum, and rpm for secure and efficient package updates.
content: Use dnf (Dandified Yum) for package management on RHEL 8+. Understand security updates, kernel patches, and dependency resolution.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo 'Checking for updates...'
sudo dnf check-update
echo 'Installing security updates...'
sudo dnf update -y --security
echo 'Listing installed packages...'
rpm -qa | wc -l
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

def run_cmd(cmd):
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    return result.stdout

print('Available updates:')
print(run_cmd('sudo dnf check-update'))

title: Network & DNS Troubleshooting
description: Fix network issues using nmcli, systemctl, and DNS configuration.
content: Diagnose and fix network problems using nmcli, systemctl, and DNS tools. Configure network interfaces and resolve connectivity issues.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Network Interfaces ==='
nmcli device show
echo -e '\n=== DNS Configuration ==='
cat /etc/resolv.conf
echo -e '\n=== Network Connectivity ==='
ping -c 1 8.8.8.8
echo -e '\n=== Route Table ==='
ip route

title: Disk & Storage Management
description: Manage LVM, partitions, and storage with df, du, and mount commands.
content: Master disk management including LVM, partitions, mounting, and storage optimization. Use df, du, and lvm tools effectively.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Disk Usage ==='
df -h
echo -e '\n=== Directory Sizes ==='
du -sh /*
echo -e '\n=== LVM Volumes ==='
sudo lvs
echo -e '\n=== Physical Volumes ==='
sudo pvs
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import psutil

disk = psutil.disk_usage('/')
print(f'Total: {disk.total / (1024**3):.2f}GB')
print(f'Used: {disk.used / (1024**3):.2f}GB')
print(f'Free: {disk.free / (1024**3):.2f}GB')
print(f'Percent: {disk.percent}%')

title: Users, Permissions & SELinux
description: Control access with useradd, chmod, and SELinux policies.
content: Manage user accounts, file permissions, and SELinux security policies. Implement least privilege access controls.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Creating User ==='
sudo useradd -m -s /bin/bash newuser
echo -e '\n=== Setting Permissions ==='
sudo chmod 755 /home/newuser
echo -e '\n=== SELinux Status ==='
getenforce
echo -e '\n=== User Groups ==='
groups newuser

title: Performance Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose and fix performance issues using top, iostat, and vmstat.
content: Identify performance bottlenecks using system monitoring tools. Analyze CPU, memory, disk I/O, and network performance.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== CPU Info ==='
cat /proc/cpuinfo | grep processor | wc -l
echo -e '\n=== Load Average ==='
cat /proc/loadavg
echo -e '\n=== Disk I/O ==='
iostat -x 1 3
echo -e '\n=== Memory Stats ==='
vmstat 1 3
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import psutil
import os

print('Performance Metrics:')
print(f'CPU Count: {psutil.cpu_count()}')
print(f'CPU Percent: {psutil.cpu_percent(interval=1)}%')
print(f'Load Average: {os.getloadavg()}')
mem = psutil.virtual_memory()
print(f'Memory Available: {mem.available / (1024**3):.2f}GB')

title: Service & Process Management
description: Control services with systemctl and manage processes effectively.
content: Use systemctl to manage systemd services. Start, stop, enable, and disable services. Monitor process status.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Service Status ==='
sudo systemctl status sshd
echo -e '\n=== Active Services ==='
sudo systemctl list-units --type=service --state=running
echo -e '\n=== Process List ==='
ps aux | head -10

title: Backups & Recovery
description: Implement reliable backups using rsync, tar, and restore procedures.
content: Create and manage backups using rsync and tar. Implement recovery procedures and test backup integrity.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Creating Backup ==='
tar -czf backup_$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz /home/user/important
echo -e '\n=== Listing Backups ==='
ls -lh backup_*.tar.gz
echo -e '\n=== Rsync Backup ==='
rsync -avz /home/user/important /backup/
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import tarfile
import os
from datetime import datetime

backup_name = f'backup_{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.tar.gz'
with tarfile.open(backup_name, 'w:gz') as tar:
    tar.add('/home/user/important')
print(f'Backup created: {backup_name}')

title: Hardware Issues on Physical Servers
description: Diagnose hardware problems with lspci, dmidecode, and sensors.
content: Identify and troubleshoot hardware issues. Use system tools to check CPU, memory, disks, and other components.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== PCI Devices ==='
lspci
echo -e '\n=== System Information ==='
dmidecode | head -20
echo -e '\n=== Temperature Sensors ==='
sensors
echo -e '\n=== Disk Health ==='
sudo smartctl -a /dev/sda
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

def run_cmd(cmd):
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    return result.stdout

print('System Hardware:')
print(run_cmd('lspci | head -10'))

title: AWS EC2 & SSM Patch Manager
description: Manage RHEL instances on AWS with EC2, SSM, and patch automation.
content: Deploy and manage RHEL on AWS EC2. Use Systems Manager for patching and automation.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== EC2 Instance Info ==='
curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id
echo -e '\n=== Availability Zone ==='
curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/placement/availability-zone
echo -e '\n=== Security Groups ==='
aws ec2 describe-security-groups --query 'SecurityGroups[*].[GroupName]'

title: Fresh RHEL 9 Installation
description: Complete guide to installing RHEL 9 on physical servers and AWS EC2.
content: Step-by-step guide for fresh RHEL 9 installation. Covers physical servers and AWS EC2 deployment.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== RHEL 9 Installation Guide ==='
echo 'Step 1: Boot from RHEL 9 ISO'
echo 'Step 2: Select installation destination'
echo 'Step 3: Configure network'
echo 'Step 4: Set root password'
echo 'Step 5: Create user account'
echo 'Step 6: Begin installation'
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
print('RHEL 9 Installation Checklist')
steps = [
    'Boot from ISO',
    'Select disk',
    'Configure network',
    'Set passwords',
    'Create users',
    'Install system'
]
for i, step in enumerate(steps, 1):
    print(f'{i}. {step}')

title: RHEL 8 to 9 Major Upgrade
description: Safely upgrade from RHEL 8 to 9 using Leapp with zero downtime.
content: Use Leapp tool for in-place upgrade from RHEL 8 to 9. Minimize downtime and ensure compatibility.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Pre-Upgrade Checks ==='
sudo leapp preupgrade
echo -e '\n=== Fixing Issues ==='
sudo leapp answer --section remove_pam_pkcs11_module_check=True
echo -e '\n=== Starting Upgrade ==='
sudo leapp upgrade
echo -e '\n=== Reboot ==='
sudo reboot

title: Automated Patching with Satellite
description: Set up Red Hat Satellite for centralized patch management.
content: Configure Red Hat Satellite for automated patching across RHEL systems.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Register with Satellite ==='
sudo subscription-manager register --org=MyOrg --activationkey=mykey
echo -e '\n=== Enable Satellite Repos ==='
sudo subscription-manager repos --enable=rhel-*-satellite-tools-*
echo -e '\n=== Install Satellite Tools ==='
sudo dnf install -y osad
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

print('Satellite Registration')
print('1. Register system')
print('2. Enable repos')
print('3. Install tools')

title: Firewall Configuration with firewalld
description: Configure firewall rules using firewalld on RHEL.
content: Manage firewall rules with firewalld. Open/close ports, configure zones, and implement security policies.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Firewall Status ==='
sudo firewall-cmd --state
echo -e '\n=== List Rules ==='
sudo firewall-cmd --list-all
echo -e '\n=== Open Port ==='
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-port=8080/tcp
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

def firewall_cmd(args):
    cmd = f'sudo firewall-cmd {args}'
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    return result.stdout

print(firewall_cmd('--state'))

title: SSH Key Management & Security
description: Secure SSH access with key-based authentication.
content: Configure SSH keys, disable password auth, and implement security best practices.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Generate SSH Key ==='
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
echo -e '\n=== Copy to Server ==='
ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub user@server
echo -e '\n=== Test SSH ==='
ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 user@server 'echo Connected'
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess
import os

key_file = os.path.expanduser('~/.ssh/id_ed25519')
if not os.path.exists(key_file):
    print('Generating SSH key...')
    subprocess.run(['ssh-keygen', '-t', 'ed25519', '-f', key_file])

title: Container Management with Podman
description: Run and manage containers using Podman on RHEL.
content: Use Podman for container management. Pull images, run containers, and manage container lifecycle.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Install Podman ==='
sudo dnf install -y podman
echo -e '\n=== Pull Image ==='
podman pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi
echo -e '\n=== Run Container ==='
podman run -it registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi /bin/bash
echo -e '\n=== List Containers ==='
podman ps -a
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

def podman_cmd(args):
    cmd = f'podman {args}'
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    return result.stdout

print('Podman Images:')
print(podman_cmd('images'))

title: Kernel Tuning & Performance
description: Optimize kernel parameters for performance.
content: Tune kernel parameters using sysctl for optimal performance.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== View Kernel Parameters ==='
sysctl -a | grep net.core
echo -e '\n=== Set Parameter ==='
sudo sysctl -w net.core.somaxconn=65535
echo -e '\n=== Persist Changes ==='
echo 'net.core.somaxconn=65535' | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

def get_sysctl(param):
    cmd = f'sysctl {param}'
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    return result.stdout

print(get_sysctl('net.core.somaxconn'))

title: Systemd Service Creation
description: Create and manage custom systemd services.
content: Write systemd service files for custom applications.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Create Service File ==='
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/myapp.service > /dev/null <<EOF
[Unit]
Description=My Application
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=myuser
WorkingDirectory=/opt/myapp
ExecStart=/opt/myapp/start.sh
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
echo -e '\n=== Enable Service ==='
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable myapp.service
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

service_content = '''[Unit]
Description=Python App
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/python3 /opt/app/main.py
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
'''

with open('/etc/systemd/system/pyapp.service', 'w') as f:
    f.write(service_content)
print('Service file created')

title: Log Rotation with logrotate
description: Configure automatic log rotation to manage disk space.
content: Set up logrotate to automatically rotate and compress logs.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Create Logrotate Config ==='
sudo tee /etc/logrotate.d/myapp > /dev/null <<EOF
/var/log/myapp/*.log {
    daily
    rotate 7
    compress
    delaycompress
    notifempty
    create 0640 myuser mygroup
    sharedscripts
}
EOF
echo -e '\n=== Test Logrotate ==='
sudo logrotate -d /etc/logrotate.d/myapp
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import subprocess
from datetime import datetime

log_file = '/var/log/myapp/app.log'
if os.path.getsize(log_file) > 100*1024*1024:  # 100MB
    backup = f'{log_file}.{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}'
    os.rename(log_file, backup)
    print(f'Log rotated to {backup}')

title: SELinux Policy Management
description: Manage SELinux policies for security.
content: Configure and troubleshoot SELinux policies.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== SELinux Status ==='
getenforce
echo -e '\n=== Set to Enforcing ==='
sudo setenforce 1
echo -e '\n=== Check Violations ==='
sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent
echo -e '\n=== Generate Policy ==='
sudo audit2allow -a -M mymodule

title: NFS Mount Configuration
description: Configure NFS mounts for network storage.
content: Set up NFS client and mount remote filesystems.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Install NFS Client ==='
sudo dnf install -y nfs-utils
echo -e '\n=== Create Mount Point ==='
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/nfs
echo -e '\n=== Mount NFS ==='
sudo mount -t nfs server:/export /mnt/nfs
echo -e '\n=== Persistent Mount ==='
echo 'server:/export /mnt/nfs nfs defaults 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess
import os

mount_point = '/mnt/nfs'
if not os.path.ismount(mount_point):
    cmd = f'sudo mount -t nfs server:/export {mount_point}'
    subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True)
    print(f'Mounted NFS at {mount_point}')

title: Certificate Management with certbot
description: Obtain and manage SSL/TLS certificates.
content: Use certbot to get free SSL certificates from Let's Encrypt.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Install Certbot ==='
sudo dnf install -y certbot python3-certbot-nginx
echo -e '\n=== Get Certificate ==='
sudo certbot certonly --nginx -d example.com
echo -e '\n=== Auto Renewal ==='
sudo systemctl enable certbot-renew.timer
python_script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import subprocess

def get_certificate(domain):
    cmd = f'sudo certbot certonly --standalone -d {domain}'
    subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True)
    print(f'Certificate obtained for {domain}')

get_certificate('example.com')

title: Monitoring with Prometheus & Grafana
description: Set up system monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana.
content: Deploy Prometheus for metrics collection and Grafana for visualization.
bash_script:
#!/bin/bash
echo '=== Install Prometheus ==='
sudo dnf install -y prometheus
echo -e '\n=== Start Service ==='
sudo systemctl start prometheus
sudo systemctl enable prometheus
echo -e '\n=== Install Grafana ==='
sudo dnf install -y grafana-server
sudo systemctl start grafana-server
